summary: IP Global Terminal (IPGT) — a cryptographic timestamping, certification, and dispute facilitation layer. The platform builds interoperable infrastructure that strengthens IP systems, speeds up IP registration, and offers automated filing strategy optimization, SME-first infrastructure, and open cryptographic auditing layers. WIPO/Sovereign IP integration references have been removed. The app features an ONDO Finance-inspired dark navy/gold landing page with sections for How It Works, Automated Filing Strategy, SME Infrastructure, and Open Cryptographic Auditing. A separate Global IP Database tab provides a searchable interface with blockchain metadata and mock IP records. The updated whitepaper covers cryptographic timestamping focus. All cryptocurrency/coin language and all WIPO references have been removed; blockchain is framed as infrastructure. Hypothetical statistics (e.g. '180+ jurisdictions served') are also removed.
architectural_notes: Platform runs on ICP (Internet Computer Protocol) using canisters as smart contracts
IP records include: title, description, category (patent/trademark/copyright), owner identity, registration date, document hash, jurisdiction
Document uploads (PDFs, images) supported; file hash recorded on-chain regardless of upload
Global IP Database pre-seeded with mock IP records for demo/search purposes; blockchain metadata displayed per record

existing_features.title: Integrate Internet Identity authentication into the frontend. Display a login/logout button. When logged in, show the user's principal ID and their IPGT coin balance fetched from the backend. Unauthenticated users can browse and search the IP database but cannot register an IP.

Build an IP Registration page in the frontend. The form must include fields for: title (text), description (textarea), category (select: Patent, Trademark, Copyright), jurisdiction (text), document hash (text, optional if file is uploaded), and a file upload input accepting PDFs and images. On submission, compute and display the SHA-256 hash of any uploaded file client-side, call the backend registration function, and show a success confirmation with the assigned IP ID and the amount of IPGT coins burned. Display a clear error if the user has insufficient IPGT balance.

Build a Global IP Database page in the frontend. Display a searchable, paginated table/card list of all registered IPs. Provide filter/search controls for: keyword (searches title), category (dropdown), and jurisdiction (text). Each IP entry should display: IP ID, title, category, jurisdiction, owner (truncated principal), registration date, and document hash. Include a detail view or modal for each IP showing all fields including the full document hash.

Build an IPGT Coin dashboard section in the frontend (accessible after login). Display: the user's current IPGT balance, the current total circulating supply, and the total IPGT burned to date. Include a simple transfer form allowing the user to send IPGT coins to another principal.

Apply a cohesive, professional visual theme across the entire frontend. The design should evoke a global, authoritative, and high-tech intellectual property registry — think dark navy/charcoal backgrounds with gold and white accents, bold serif or semi-serif headings, clean data-dense layouts, and subtle blockchain/document motifs. Enforce this theme consistently across all pages: navigation, forms, database views, and the coin dashboard.

Generate and integrate a hero banner image for the IPGT landing/home page featuring a dark, futuristic globe with glowing gold circuit-line overlays evoking a global blockchain network, and the branding text area left blank for overlay use.
Generate and integrate an IPGT logo mark — a stylized globe or shield icon with a golden 'IP' monogram — used in the navigation bar and favicon.

Update the IP Registration page (frontend/src/pages/RegisterIP.tsx) and the RegistrationSuccessModal (frontend/src/components/RegistrationSuccessModal.tsx) to display the correct burn fee of 0.02 IPGT instead of 100 IPGT. Any UI text, tooltips, or helper copy referencing the burn amount must reflect the new fee.

Create a new Whitepaper page at frontend/src/pages/Whitepaper.tsx. The page must be a long, detailed, professionally written whitepaper rendered as a styled in-app page. It must include all of the following sections with extensive prose under each: (1) Executive Summary — IPGT's mission to decentralize and democratize IP protection globally on the Internet Computer; (2) The Problem — fragmented, expensive, jurisdiction-locked, and inaccessible traditional IP registration systems worldwide; (3) The Solution — IPGT's on-chain, tamper-proof IP registry with open access via Internet Identity; (4) Technology Architecture — ICP canisters, on-chain state, Internet Identity, immutable records, canister upgrades and stable memory; (5) Encryption & Hashing Methodology — SHA-256 document fingerprinting computed client-side, how it proves prior art and establishes ownership without storing the document itself, and how hashes are stored immutably on-chain; (6) Duplicate IP Protection — the system enforces uniqueness by rejecting any registration attempt where the document hash already exists on-chain, making double-registration of the same IP impossible; (7) IPGT Tokenomics — 100B total supply, 25B released immediately at launch, 75B vesting linearly over 48 months (~1.5625B IPGT/month), burn-on-registration mechanism with 0.02 IPGT burned per registration, deflationary supply dynamics; (8) Token Release Schedule — month-by-month breakdown table and rationale covering ecosystem growth, anti-inflation design, long-term sustainability, and ICPSwap liquidity strategy; (9) Use Cases — Patents, Trademarks, Copyrights, creative works, software, trade secrets, media, and domain innovations; (10) Roadmap — Phase 1 (platform launch and initial IP registrations), Phase 2 (ICPSwap listing and token liquidity), Phase 3 (global adoption, API access, institutional partnerships); (11) Legal Disclaimer — the platform does not constitute legal IP protection in any jurisdiction and users should consult qualified legal counsel. The page must include a sticky left-sidebar table of contents with anchor links to each section, smooth scroll behavior, and a print-to-PDF button at the top of the page. Style must fully match the existing dark navy/gold theme (Playfair Display headings, Inter body, gold accents, charcoal/navy backgrounds).
Add the Whitepaper route to the TanStack Router tree in frontend/src/App.tsx. Register a route at /whitepaper pointing to the new Whitepaper page component.
Update frontend/src/components/Header.tsx to add a 'Whitepaper' navigation link pointing to /whitepaper alongside the existing nav links, maintaining the current dark navy/gold header styling.

Update frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx to add a prominent 'Read the Whitepaper' call-to-action button or card in the feature highlights / CTA section. The button should link to /whitepaper and use the existing gold accent button style.

Restore frontend/src/pages/IPDatabase.tsx, frontend/src/pages/RegisterIP.tsx, and frontend/src/pages/CoinDashboard.tsx to their pre-whitepaper visual state — reverting any layout, typography, color, or spacing changes that were introduced as a side effect of the whitepaper feature implementation.

In frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx, increase the visual contrast on all insight/feature highlight boxes: apply dark card backgrounds (e.g. deep navy or charcoal, using existing --color-navy or --color-charcoal tokens) with clearly visible gold or subtle light borders, and ensure all card text uses gold or off-white colors so that font content stands out strongly against the card background. No card or insight box should have a background color that blends with its text color.

Audit all non-Whitepaper frontend pages and shared components (Home.tsx, IPDatabase.tsx, RegisterIP.tsx, CoinDashboard.tsx, Header.tsx, Footer.tsx, Layout.tsx, IPCard.tsx, IPDetailModal.tsx, TokenStats.tsx, ProfileSetupModal.tsx, RegistrationSuccessModal.tsx) and eliminate all white or near-white backgrounds, card fills, and surface colors. Replace every white surface with the appropriate dark navy or charcoal tone from the existing theme tokens. The Whitepaper page (frontend/src/pages/Whitepaper.tsx) must remain completely untouched.

Replace the existing hero banner image with a newly generated sharper, more vivid version. The new image must feature a dark futuristic globe with highly detailed glowing gold circuit-line and network node overlays on a deep space/dark navy background, evoking a global blockchain network. The image must be rendered at 1920×900px and integrated in frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx as the hero background, replacing the current hero image asset.

Rebuild frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx as an ONDO Finance-inspired landing page for IP Global Terminal. The page must include the following sections in order: (1) Hero — bold headline positioning IPGT as a cryptographic timestamping, certification, and dispute facilitation layer; subtitle copy referencing WIPO and Sovereign IP integration; a primary CTA button; retain the existing dark futuristic globe hero background image. (2) How It Works — 3–4 step process showing how IP is submitted, hashed, timestamped on-chain, and certified. (3) Integration with WIPO & Sovereign IP — section explaining interoperability with WIPO and Sovereign IP registries, framed as infrastructure that strengthens existing systems. (4) Automated Filing Strategy — section on optimization tools for IP filing workflows and strategy. (5) SME-First Infrastructure — section highlighting accessible, affordable IP protection infrastructure for small and medium enterprises. (6) Open Cryptographic Auditing — section on transparent, verifiable on-chain audit trails and open audit layers. (7) CTA — call-to-action directing users to the IP Database and Whitepaper. Remove all cryptocurrency, coin, token balance, and IPGT-token language from the homepage. Do not show TokenStats or any coin dashboard references. Maintain the dark navy/gold theme with Playfair Display headings, Inter body text, and gold accents. The overall aesthetic and layout should feel clean, institutional, and premium — similar to ONDO Finance.

Update frontend/src/pages/IPDatabase.tsx to be accessible on a dedicated separate route (e.g. /database) from the landing page. Pre-seed the database display with at least 8 realistic mock IP records covering Patents, Trademarks, and Copyrights across multiple jurisdictions. Each mock record must include: IP ID, title, category, jurisdiction, owner (truncated principal), registration date, document hash (SHA-256 format), and blockchain transaction metadata fields (block height, canister ID, timestamp). Display these mock records alongside any real on-chain records. The page must remain searchable and filterable by title keyword, category, and jurisdiction as it currently is. Blockchain metadata fields must be visible in the detail modal for each record.

Rewrite frontend/src/pages/Whitepaper.tsx to fully reflect the new IP Global Terminal positioning. The updated whitepaper must cover: (1) Executive Summary — IPGT's mission as a cryptographic timestamping, certification, and dispute facilitation layer that integrates with Sovereign IP and WIPO to strengthen global IP systems. (2) The Problem — fragmented, expensive, jurisdiction-locked traditional IP registration; lack of interoperability between national IP offices; barriers for SMEs. (3) The Solution — IPGT's on-chain tamper-proof cryptographic timestamping and certification infrastructure, open to any registrant globally. (4) Technology Architecture — ICP canisters, on-chain state, Internet Identity, immutable records, SHA-256 hashing, canister upgrades and stable memory. (5) Encryption & Hashing Methodology — SHA-256 document fingerprinting computed client-side, proving prior art and establishing ownership without storing the document. (6) Duplicate IP Protection — uniqueness enforced by rejecting registrations with an existing document hash on-chain. (7) WIPO & Sovereign IP Integration — how IPGT interoperates with WIPO and Sovereign IP registries to serve as a cryptographic pre-registration and dispute evidence layer. (8) Automated Filing Strategy Optimization — how IPGT's infrastructure automates and optimizes IP filing workflows for applicants. (9) SME-First Infrastructure — accessibility, low cost, and tooling designed for small and medium enterprises. (10) Open Cryptographic Auditing — transparent, publicly verifiable on-chain audit trails. (11) Roadmap — Phase 1 (platform launch and initial IP timestamping), Phase 2 (WIPO/Sovereign IP integration pilots), Phase 3 (global adoption, API access, institutional partnerships). (12) Legal Disclaimer — the platform does not constitute legal IP protection in any jurisdiction and users should consult qualified legal counsel. Remove all cryptocurrency, token, coin, tokenomics, token release schedule, and ICPSwap language. Replace all blockchain framing with blockchain-as-infrastructure narrative. Retain the sticky left-sidebar table of contents with anchor links, smooth scroll, print-to-PDF button, and the existing dark navy/gold theme (Playfair Display headings, Inter body, gold accents).

Update frontend/src/components/Header.tsx to reflect the new IP Global Terminal navigation structure. Nav links should point to: Home (/), IP Database (/database), Whitepaper (/whitepaper). Remove any navigation links or UI elements referencing the Coin Dashboard, IPGT token balance display, or cryptocurrency features. Retain the IPGT logo, dark navy/gold styling, login/logout button for Internet Identity, and authenticated user principal display. If the user is authenticated, show their display name or principal but do not show a coin balance.

Update frontend/src/App.tsx to register the route for the IP Database at /database (in addition to or replacing the existing /ip-database route if one exists). Ensure the Whitepaper route remains registered at /whitepaper. Remove or de-register the Coin Dashboard route from the main navigation flow. The Home route must remain at /.

Update frontend/src/components/Footer.tsx to remove all cryptocurrency, coin, token, and IPGT token language. Update footer copy and navigation links to reflect the new IP Global Terminal positioning (cryptographic timestamping, certification, dispute facilitation, WIPO/Sovereign IP integration). Retain the dark navy/gold theme and copyright line.

In frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx, remove every reference to WIPO (e.g. any section titled 'Integration with WIPO & Sovereign IP', any subtitle copy mentioning WIPO or Sovereign IP integration) and remove all hypothetical or unverified statistics (e.g. '180+ jurisdictions served', any fabricated numeric claims). Do not remove or alter the Mock IP records, the How It Works section, Automated Filing Strategy, SME-First Infrastructure, Open Cryptographic Auditing, or any other content. Preserve the dark navy/gold theme, layout, and all remaining section content exactly as-is.

In frontend/src/pages/Whitepaper.tsx, remove every reference to WIPO, Sovereign IP integration, and any hypothetical or unverified statistics. This includes removing or rewriting the 'WIPO & Sovereign IP Integration' section (section 7 in the current whitepaper) and any inline mentions of WIPO, sovereign IP registries, or fabricated metrics throughout the document. Replace removed WIPO/Sovereign IP content with a neutral section on 'Global IP Ecosystem Interoperability' framed purely as blockchain-as-infrastructure without naming WIPO or any sovereign body. Retain all other sections, the sticky left-sidebar table of contents (updating anchor links to reflect any section title changes), smooth scroll, print-to-PDF button, and the existing dark navy/gold theme.

In frontend/src/components/Footer.tsx, remove every reference to WIPO and Sovereign IP integration (including any footer copy, taglines, or navigation links mentioning WIPO). Also remove any hypothetical statistics if present. Do not change the footer's dark navy/gold theme, copyright line, or remaining navigation links (Home, IP Database, Whitepaper).

new_feature_requests.summary: Global IP Database — publicly searchable and browsable, connected to ICP canisters

Implement a publicly accessible IP database query layer in the backend. Provide functions to: list all registered IPs (paginated), search IPs by title keyword, category, jurisdiction, and owner principal, and retrieve a single IP record by its unique ID. These query functions must be callable without authentication.

Add a long, detailed, professionally styled in-app whitepaper page accessible from the homepage. Sections include: executive summary, the problem, the solution, technology architecture, encryption/hashing methodology, duplicate IP protection, IPGT tokenomics, token release schedule with rationale, use cases, roadmap, and legal disclaimer. Match dark navy/gold theme with sticky TOC sidebar, section anchors, and print-to-PDF button.

Restore frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx to its pre-whitepaper visual state (hero banner, feature grid, navigation buttons, color scheme, typography, spacing, and layout) exactly as it appeared before the whitepaper feature was added, while retaining the 'Read the Whitepaper' call-to-action button or card that links to /whitepaper using the existing gold accent button style.

Restore frontend/src/components/Header.tsx to its pre-whitepaper visual state (logo placement, nav link styling, authenticated balance display, mobile menu) while keeping the existing 'Whitepaper' navigation link that was already added.

Restore frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx and frontend/src/components/Footer.tsx to their pre-whitepaper visual state, reverting any structural or styling changes introduced by the whitepaper feature while preserving the whitepaper route in App.tsx.

Audit frontend/src/index.css and frontend/tailwind.config.js for any global style changes introduced by the whitepaper feature implementation that affect non-whitepaper pages, and revert only those changes while preserving whitepaper-specific print styles and prose utilities.

Increase contrast on Home page insight boxes (gold/light text on dark card backgrounds with visible borders), remove all white backgrounds/cards/fills across non-whitepaper pages (replace with dark navy/charcoal), and regenerate/enhance the hero background image to be sharper and more vivid while keeping the dark futuristic globe with glowing gold circuit-line overlays theme.

Rebuild homepage as ONDO Finance-inspired landing page with sections: Hero, How It Works, WIPO/Sovereign IP Integration, Automated Filing Strategy, SME Infrastructure, Open Cryptographic Auditing, and CTA. Remove all cryptocurrency/coin language. Keep dark navy/gold theme.

Move Global IP Database to a separate tab/route from the landing page. Pre-seed with mock IP records showing blockchain data (IP ID, title, category, jurisdiction, owner, registration date, document hash, blockchain transaction metadata). Make it searchable.

Rewrite the Whitepaper page to reflect new positioning: cryptographic timestamping, WIPO/Sovereign IP integration, dispute facilitation, automated filing strategy, SME-first infrastructure, open cryptographic auditing. Remove cryptocurrency/coin framing, replace with blockchain-as-infrastructure narrative. Keep same format and theme.

Remove all WIPO references and hypothetical statistics (e.g. '180+ jurisdictions served') from every page and section — Hero, homepage sections, Whitepaper, Footer, and anywhere else they appear. Keep all Mock IP records intact.

Audit frontend/src/components/Header.tsx, frontend/src/pages/IPDatabase.tsx, frontend/src/pages/RegisterIP.tsx, and any other frontend files not already covered above for any remaining references to WIPO, Sovereign IP integration, or hypothetical statistics (e.g. '180+ jurisdictions served'). Remove all such references. Do not modify Mock IP record data, functionality, or visual styling of any component.
